---
title: "Professor Claude"
subtitle: "A personal applied math & statistics school"
toc: false
---

Welcome. This site hosts **example courses** that teach the fundamentals behind a
statistical method or math concept, working up from building blocks to the real
thing — with toy numerical examples, plain-English notation decoding, and
**runnable in-browser R (WebR) cells**. No install needed.

## How to use this site

- **Start in [Foundations](foundations/README.md)** if you want reusable building
  blocks (vectors and summation, derivatives, likelihood, and so on). Each module
  has a **Lesson**, **Practice** problems with hidden answers, and curated
  **Resources**.
- **Pick a course** from the sidebar to see those blocks assembled into a full
  method — each opens with a **syllabus** and a **roadmap** showing what depends
  on what, then runs module by module to a **capstone**.
- **Run the code.** Cells marked live execute in your browser — edit them, hit
  **Run**, and see the output change. Nothing to set up.
- **Read in order.** Lessons follow a fixed spine (intuition → worked example →
  notation → derivation → practice → self-check); working top to bottom is the
  intended path.

## Feedback welcome

This is an open project — issues, corrections, and suggestions are genuinely
welcome. Open one on
**[GitHub](https://github.com/radlinsky/professor-claude/issues)**.

The whole system is designed to be **retargeted to your own learner** and to
generate new courses on demand — see the
[README](https://github.com/radlinsky/professor-claude#make-it-yours) for how to
make it yours.
